display.purpose: Brand moments, hero numbers on the Today signal hero, the wordmark itself. Used sparingly.

title.purpose: Card headers and section titles inside pages.

body.purpose: The working text. Cap line length at 65–75ch for prose blocks.

label.purpose: Eyebrow labels, table headers, ScienceNote section markers (WHY THIS / METHODOLOGY / CITATION). JetBrains Mono uppercase.

data.purpose: Every numeric value. JetBrains Mono with tabular-nums. Applied via the .font-data utility.

shadows.name: popover-lift
shadows.purpose: Lift for floating surfaces (Dialog, DropdownMenu, Tooltip). State-driven, never ambient on cards.

shadows.name: hover-soft
shadows.purpose: Optional hover lift on interactive cards. Apply only when the card itself is the click target.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions. Exponential ease-out per impeccable shared laws — no bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-slow
motion.purpose: Layered transitions (Dialog open, sheet slide). Reduce to 0 under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Field Green action button. One per surface, ideally one per page.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); color: oklch(0.975 0.008 85); padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5; letter-spacing: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.35 0.14 155); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.16 155); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Default secondary action. No background at rest; fills with rule-soft on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.40 0.02 260); padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.92 0.006 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.55 0.16 155); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Signal Badge
components.description: Today's training signal. Three semantic variants: positive (Go, green), caution (Modify, amber), rest (Rest, rust).
components.css: .ds-badge-go, .ds-badge-modify, .ds-badge-rest { display: inline-block; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; margin-right: 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-badge-go { background: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); color: oklch(0.975 0.008 85); } .ds-badge-modify { background: oklch(0.72 0.15 75); color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); } .ds-badge-rest { background: oklch(0.55 0.18 30); color: oklch(0.975 0.008 85); }

components.name: Card
components.description: The default container. Sits flat on warm paper with a hairline rule-soft border. No ambient shadows.
components.html:
WEEKLY LOAD
42.6 km
+8% vs 4-wk avg
components.css: .ds-card { background: oklch(0.99 0.004 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); padding: 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.625rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.006 85); font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 280px; } .ds-card-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.40 0.02 260); margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .ds-card-value { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-card-unit { font-size: 1rem; color: oklch(0.60 0.015 260); margin-left: 0.25rem; } .ds-card-meta { margin-top: 0.5rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Input field. Rule border at rest; primary on focus (no glow ring). Caret in primary.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(0.99 0.004 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.85 0.008 85); border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; caret-color: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); width: 220px; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.60 0.015 260); } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; }

components.name: Science Note
components.description: Signature reasoning surface. The cobalt 3px left border is reserved for this component alone — it is the brand's commitment to methodology made visible.
components.html:
WHY THIS RECOMMENDATION

Today's GO signal is grounded in your 7-day HRV trend (+4.2% above baseline) and last-night sleep score (84). Recent 7-day load is within +/-10% of your 28-day chronic load, indicating adequate training stress without accumulated fatigue.

Banister et al., 1975. A systems model of training for athletic performance.
components.css: .ds-science-note { background: color-mix(in oklab, oklch(0.50 0.18 258) 5%, oklch(0.99 0.004 85)); border-left: 3px solid oklch(0.50 0.18 258); border-top: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, oklch(0.50 0.18 258) 15%, oklch(0.92 0.006 85)); border-right: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, oklch(0.50 0.18 258) 15%, oklch(0.92 0.006 85)); border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, oklch(0.50 0.18 258) 15%, oklch(0.92 0.006 85)); border-radius: 0.5rem; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 480px; } .ds-science-eyebrow { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.50 0.18 258); margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .ds-science-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; color: oklch(0.40 0.02 260); margin: 0 0 0.75rem 0; } .ds-science-citation { padding-top: 0.5rem; border-top: 1px dashed oklch(0.85 0.008 85); font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(0.60 0.015 260); } .ds-science-citation em { font-style: italic; } .ds-science-citation a { color: oklch(0.50 0.18 258); text-decoration: none; } .ds-science-citation a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

components.name: Sidebar Item
components.description: Sidebar navigation row. Active state shown by primary left-edge indicator and bolder weight, not background fill.
components.html: TodayTrainingGoalHistory
components.css: .ds-sidebar { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.125rem; padding: 0.5rem; background: oklch(0.97 0.005 80); border-right: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.006 85); width: 200px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', 'DM Sans Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-sidebar-item { padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; padding-left: 1rem; color: oklch(0.40 0.02 260); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; border-left: 2px solid transparent; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-sidebar-item:hover { background: oklch(0.92 0.006 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); } .ds-sidebar-item-active { color: oklch(0.18 0.02 260); font-weight: 600; border-left-color: oklch(0.55 0.16 155); }

narrative.overview:
Praxys looks like a rigorous instrument that travels outdoors with you. Warm paper instead of clinical white. Precise typography instead of marketing flourish. Citations in the margins, not in a footer. The interface respects that the reader is a person making real decisions about real training, often in real sunlight, often with a sweaty thumb. It does not perform.

The system has two voices, separated by color: green carries action — follow this plan, run today, this is your signal — and cobalt carries reasoning — this is why, this is the paper, this is the methodology. The user always knows whether they are being told to do or being shown why. The two-track palette is the design system's central commitment; everything else flows from it.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper surfaces (oklch(0.975 0.008 85)), never #fff. Light theme is the default — runners use this in sunlight.
Two-track semantic palette: green for action, cobalt for reasoning. Amber for caution. Rust for rest and high-intensity ('this costs you').
Tabular monospace numerals everywhere a number lives. Column alignment is the point.
Flat-by-default surfaces; thin rules separate; elevation is a response to state, not ambient decoration.
Bilingual (EN + 中文) is structural, with three explicit modes — never always-both by reflex.

rules.name: The Two-Track Rule
rules.body: Green means act; cobalt means the system explaining itself. Never blur them. A green ScienceNote is a bug. A cobalt CTA button is a bug.

rules.name: The Warm Paper Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are tinted toward 85° hue at low chroma (≤0.012). Pure #fff and pure #000 are forbidden — they are clinical and they punish the eye in real sunlight.

rules.name: The Restraint Rule
rules.body: Primary green appears on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point. Plastering green everywhere flattens its meaning into chrome.

rules.name: The Rust = Cost Rule
rules.body: Rust marks rest and high-intensity zones with the same color because both say this costs you. Recovery is not a soft message; it is the system stopping you from breaking yourself.

rules.name: The Tabular Numerals Rule
rules.body: Every digit on every screen renders in JetBrains Mono with tabular-nums. Mixing proportional and tabular numerals across a column is a bug.

rules.name: The Right Word Rule
rules.body: Use the technical term — threshold, CTL, polarized, RPE, zone 2 — and explain it inline once, the first time it appears in a flow. Don't dumb it down.

rules.name: The Section Label Rule
rules.body: Section labels are JetBrains Mono, uppercase, tracking 0.05em, in ink-soft. They are eyebrows above content blocks.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows are responses, not decoration. If you can't articulate which user state caused the shadow, remove it.

rules.name: The Rule-Line Rule
rules.body: Use rule-soft (hairline) to separate flat surfaces. Use rule only where the boundary needs to be unmistakable.

rules.name: The Reasoning Rail Rule
rules.body: Only the ScienceNote earns the cobalt 3px left border. The rail is reserved; that's why it's recognizable.

rules.name: The One Primary Rule
rules.body: A surface has at most one primary green button. Two competing primaries means the user has to choose between two action paths.

dos: Do use oklch() for color tokens; the project has an OKLCH-only doctrine. Hex appears only where downstream tools require it (chart libraries, miniapp WXSS).
Do apply .font-data to every numeric value, including digits embedded inside body prose.
Do reserve the cobalt 3px left border for the ScienceNote and nothing else.
Do keep one primary green button per surface. Other actions are ghost.
Do treat the light theme as the default; design for outdoor / sunlight legibility first.
Do codify bilingual treatment by surface purpose: brand surfaces always-both, product chrome locale-only, marketing primary-with-subtitle.
Do lead every dashboard screen with an interpretation — a signal, a verdict, a recommendation. The data underneath supports the interpretation; it does not replace it.
donts: Don't use #fff or #000 anywhere. Surfaces tint toward 85° hue (warm paper); foregrounds tint toward 260° hue (cool ink).
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. The cobalt 3px rail belongs to the ScienceNote exclusively.
Don't drift toward the crypto / wearable neon-on-black aesthetic. No glassmorphism, no animated gradients, no neon-on-black hero surfaces.
Don't assemble a generic SaaS hero-metric template — big number, small label, four supporting stats, gradient accent.
Don't ship a Garmin-Connect data dump — endless tabs, no narrative, no opinion, no methodology. Charts without a takeaway are failures.
Don't nest cards. A card inside a card is always wrong; use a horizontal rule, a section label, or a flat row instead.
Don't use Strava-style social validation tropes (kudos, achievement explosions, segment leaderboard chrome).
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text + gradient) for headings.
Don't apply ambient drop shadows on cards at rest. Elevation is a response to state.
Don't use --accent-blue or --accent-purple in new code — they are deprecated.
Don't show always-both EN + 中文 by reflex on product chrome. Surface purpose decides; the user's locale wins for app UI.
